questions.question: Explain the difference between Quality Assurance and Quality Control in software engineering.
keyPoints: QA focuses on process improvement and defect prevention
QC focuses on product testing and defect detection
QA is proactive, QC is reactive
Both are complementary activities in software quality management

questions.question: List and explain any four quality factors from the McCall Quality Model under Product Operation category.
keyPoints: Correctness: Degree to which software meets specifications
Efficiency: Resource usage relative to performance
Usability: Ease of learning and operation

questions.question: What are the six primary quality characteristics defined in ISO 9126 standard?
Define software reliability and explain two measures used to quantify it.
keyPoints: Definition: Probability of failure-free operation over time

questions.question: Explain the concept of software maintainability with reference to its key sub-characteristics.
keyPoints: Analysability: Effort to identify root cause

questions.question: What is the difference between ISO 9126 and ISO 25010 standards for software quality?
Explain the term 'Software Usability' and list its sub-characteristics.
keyPoints: Definition: Ease of use and learning

questions.question: Describe the McCall Quality Model in detail. Explain all the 11 quality factors and their organization into three categories with appropriate examples.
keyPoints: Three categories: Product Operation, Product Revision, Product Transition
Product Operation: Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Integrity, Usability
Explain each factor with real-world example

questions.question: Explain the ISO 9126 quality model. Discuss each quality characteristic and its sub-characteristics in detail.
keyPoints: Six characteristics: Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability

questions.question: Discuss software quality metrics. Explain how quality attributes are measured with examples of specific metrics for reliability and maintainability.
keyPoints: Reliability metrics: MTBF, MTTF, Failure intensity
Maintainability metrics: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR)

questions.question: Compare and contrast Quality Assurance and Quality Control. Explain how both activities contribute to achieving software quality in a software development organization.
keyPoints: QA: Process-oriented, preventive, focuses on improvement
QC: Product-oriented, detective, focuses on detection
QA activities: Process definition, audits, training
